title: Solar Power: Getting Started
content: Solar panels convert sunlight directly into electricity using photovoltaic cells. They're clean and renewable, working best in areas with high sun exposure. Solar energy production varies with time of day and weather conditions, producing peak power at noon and nothing at night.

title: Solar Technology Tier 2
content: Tier 2 solar panels use improved photovoltaic cell technology, delivering 100% more energy output than tier 1. Advanced materials and optimised panel angles maximise energy capture throughout the day.

title: Solar Technology Tier 3
content: Tier 3 solar panels deliver 150% more output than tier 1 through multi-junction cells that capture a wider spectrum of sunlight, improving performance even on overcast days.

title: Solar Technology Tier 4
content: Tier 4 panels achieve 200% more output than tier 1. Integrated micro-inverters on each panel reduce energy loss from partial shading and improve overall grid reliability.

title: Solar Technology Tier 5
content: Tier 5 solar arrays reach 250% more output than tier 1. Bifacial panels capture reflected ground light on their rear surface, boosting generation without increasing the installation footprint.

title: Solar Technology Tier 6
content: Tier 6 panels produce 300% more than tier 1. Perovskite-silicon tandem cells push efficiency beyond the Shockley-Queisser limit, a milestone previously thought unachievable in commercial solar.

title: Solar Technology Tier 7
content: Tier 7 arrays deliver 350% more output than tier 1. Concentrated photovoltaics use precision lenses to focus sunlight onto tiny high-efficiency cells, squeezing more power from the same land area.

title: Solar Technology Tier 8
content: Tier 8 panels achieve 400% more output than tier 1. Self-cleaning nano-coatings eliminate dust losses and maintenance downtime, keeping peak efficiency year-round in all climates.

title: Solar Technology Tier 9
content: Tier 9 installations generate 450% more than tier 1. Quantum dot layers tuned to infrared wavelengths harvest heat as well as light, recovering energy that conventional panels simply waste.

title: Solar Technology Tier 10
content: Tier 10 represents near-theoretical solar efficiency. Space-grade multi-junction cells capture 500% more energy than tier 1, with each panel capable of powering dozens of homes on a clear summer day.

title: Solar Achievement: 25 Installed
content: You've deployed 25 solar panels! Solar farms at this scale begin to demonstrate the viability of photovoltaic energy as a serious contributor to the grid.

title: Solar Achievement: 50 Installed
content: 50 solar panels deployed. This represents a meaningful infrastructure commitment to clean energy and measurably reduces your carbon footprint.

title: Solar Achievement: 75 Installed
content: 75 solar panels online. You're building the backbone of a renewable energy grid - enough capacity to begin balancing intermittent supply with battery storage.

title: Solar Achievement: 100 Installed
content: 100 solar panels! A solar farm of this scale can power entire neighbourhoods. Your investment in clean energy is making a real difference to atmospheric carbon levels.

title: Wind Power: Harnessing the Breeze
content: Wind turbines convert the kinetic energy of moving air into electricity through large spinning blades connected to a generator. They work best in open areas with consistent wind patterns. Output varies with wind speed and can fluctuate hour to hour.

title: Wind Technology Tier 2
content: Tier 2 turbines feature larger rotor diameters and taller towers, reaching stronger and more consistent winds. Advanced blade designs cut in at lower wind speeds, improving reliability.

title: Wind Technology Tier 3
content: Tier 3 turbines introduce carbon-fibre blades that are both lighter and stiffer. The reduced mass allows faster rotation, capturing more energy from the same wind resource.

title: Wind Technology Tier 4
content: Tier 4 turbines incorporate active blade pitch control, continuously adjusting the angle of each blade to match changing wind conditions and maintain optimal output across a wide speed range.

title: Wind Technology Tier 5
content: Tier 5 designs use direct-drive generators that eliminate the gearbox - the most failure-prone component. Fewer moving parts means lower maintenance costs and higher long-term availability.

title: Wind Technology Tier 6
content: Tier 6 turbines employ aerodynamic blade coatings inspired by whale fins, reducing turbulence drag by up to 30%. The biomimetic design proves nature often outperforms engineering intuition.

title: Wind Technology Tier 7
content: Tier 7 systems integrate real-time LIDAR wind sensors that scan conditions 200 metres ahead of the turbine. Blades pre-adjust before gusts arrive, protecting the drivetrain and smoothing output.

title: Wind Technology Tier 8
content: Tier 8 introduces floating offshore foundations that allow installation in deep water where winds are stronger and more reliable. Offshore capacity factors far exceed anything achievable on land.

title: Wind Technology Tier 9
content: Tier 9 turbines use superconducting generators that operate with near-zero electrical resistance. The result is exceptional efficiency at all wind speeds, with dramatically reduced heat loss.

title: Wind Technology Tier 10
content: Tier 10 represents the pinnacle of wind energy. Adaptive winglet tips, smart yaw drives, and AI-optimised farm layouts combine to extract the maximum possible energy from every cubic metre of moving air.

title: Wind Achievement: 25 Installed
content: 25 wind turbines commissioned. At this scale a wind farm begins contributing meaningfully to baseload supply, especially during high-wind periods.

title: Wind Achievement: 50 Installed
content: 50 turbines spinning. Your wind farm is now large enough to balance the intermittency of individual units, smoothing output considerably.

title: Wind Achievement: 75 Installed
content: 75 wind turbines operational. The sheer scale of this installation is lowering your per-unit maintenance costs and improving grid stability.

title: Wind Achievement: 100 Installed
content: 100 wind turbines! A wind farm of this magnitude is a major infrastructure achievement, capable of providing clean electricity to tens of thousands of homes.

title: Hydroelectric Power: Energy from Water
content: Hydroelectric dams generate electricity by channelling falling or flowing water through turbines connected to generators. They provide some of the most consistent and reliable renewable power available, but are limited to suitable river sites. A maximum of 10 installations can be built due to environmental constraints.

title: Hydroelectric Technology Tier 2
content: Tier 2 hydro turbines use improved runner blade geometries that reduce cavitation - the destructive bubble formation that erodes conventional turbines - extending service life and maintaining high efficiency.

title: Hydroelectric Technology Tier 3
content: Tier 3 installations add variable-speed generators that adjust output to match grid demand in real time. Unlike fixed-speed units, they can ramp from zero to full power in under 30 seconds.

title: Hydroelectric Technology Tier 4
content: Tier 4 turbines incorporate fish-friendly bypass channels and fine screening that protect aquatic ecosystems while maintaining full energy output - addressing one of hydropower's key environmental criticisms.

title: Hydroelectric Technology Tier 5
content: Tier 5 facilities employ advanced silt management systems that prevent sediment build-up behind the dam. Preserving reservoir capacity extends the operational lifespan of the installation by decades.

title: Hydroelectric Technology Tier 6
content: Tier 6 hydro units can operate in pumped-storage mode: using surplus grid electricity to pump water uphill, then releasing it to generate power on demand. This turns the dam into a giant rechargeable battery.

title: Hydroelectric Technology Tier 7
content: Tier 7 turbines introduce ceramic-composite coatings that resist abrasion from suspended sediment. Erosion that previously wore out runners in 10 years now takes 40, dramatically cutting replacement costs.

title: Hydroelectric Technology Tier 8
content: Tier 8 systems integrate AI-driven water flow forecasting using upstream sensor networks and weather models. Predictive management maximises generation while meeting downstream environmental flow requirements.

title: Hydroelectric Technology Tier 9
content: Tier 9 installations feature modular turbine clusters that can be individually maintained without shutting down the whole dam. Availability approaches 99%, making hydro one of the most reliable power sources on the grid.

title: Hydroelectric Technology Tier 10
content: Tier 10 represents the zenith of hydroelectric engineering. Ultra-low-friction bearings, lossless power electronics, and precision gate control combine to approach the theoretical Betz efficiency limit for water turbines.

title: Hydroelectric Achievement: 25 Installed
content: You've built 25 hydroelectric installations. Across your network, consistent river-powered generation is providing a stable foundation for the entire renewable grid.

title: Hydroelectric Achievement: 50 Installed
content: 50 hydroelectric dams operational. Their combined reliable output is invaluable for balancing the variability of your solar and wind assets.

title: Hydroelectric Achievement: 75 Installed
content: 75 hydroelectric installations! You're approaching the practical limits of suitable river sites, but each one is a cornerstone of long-term grid stability.

title: Hydroelectric Achievement: 100 Installed
content: 100 hydroelectric installations. This network of dams represents a massive clean energy foundation, delivering consistent power that supports every other generator type in your grid.

title: Tidal Power: Ocean Energy
content: Tidal generators harness the movement of seawater driven by the gravitational pull of the Moon and Sun - one of the most predictable energy sources on Earth. They operate in coastal areas and produce power in sync with the tidal cycle, with peak output around high and low tide.

title: Tidal Technology Tier 2
content: Tier 2 tidal turbines use larger-diameter rotors and improved bearing seals to withstand the corrosive marine environment. Greater swept area means more energy extracted from each tidal flow.

title: Tidal Technology Tier 3
content: Tier 3 installations adopt horizontal-axis turbines with variable pitch blades that harvest energy from both the flood and ebb phases of the tide, nearly doubling the productive window per cycle.

title: Tidal Technology Tier 4
content: Tier 4 devices use titanium alloy structural components that resist saltwater corrosion without the weight penalty of stainless steel, enabling installation in deeper, faster-flowing tidal channels.

title: Tidal Technology Tier 5
content: Tier 5 tidal arrays incorporate distributed mooring systems that allow turbines to align with current direction regardless of the tidal phase. Omni-directional harvesting improves annual energy yield significantly.

title: Tidal Technology Tier 6
content: Tier 6 devices use contra-rotating turbine pairs on the same shaft. The opposing rotation cancels lateral torque forces that stress conventional single-rotor mountings, extending service intervals considerably.

title: Tidal Technology Tier 7
content: Tier 7 tidal generators are integrated with real-time astronomical tide prediction models. Power output forecasts are accurate to within minutes days in advance - a predictability no other renewable energy source can match.

title: Tidal Technology Tier 8
content: Tier 8 installations use self-healing polymer coatings that repair microscopic surface cracks caused by cavitation. The technology, inspired by biological organisms, dramatically reduces maintenance diver deployments.

title: Tidal Technology Tier 9
content: Tier 9 tidal arrays incorporate piezoelectric panels on turbine towers that harvest energy from pressure fluctuations in the current. Even the structural members are contributing to total electricity generation.

title: Tidal Technology Tier 10
content: Tier 10 represents the frontier of tidal energy. Superconducting generators, biomorphic blade profiles, and smart farm layouts extract the maximum energy from every tidal cycle with minimal environmental disruption.

title: Tidal Achievement: 25 Installed
content: 25 tidal generators commissioned. Their predictable output cycle is already helping grid operators balance supply with demand more confidently.

title: Tidal Achievement: 50 Installed
content: 50 tidal installations operational. The reliable, twice-daily power pulses from this fleet are becoming an important scheduling anchor for the whole energy network.

title: Tidal Achievement: 75 Installed
content: 75 tidal generators online. You're demonstrating at scale that ocean energy is a mature, bankable technology - not just a promising prototype.

title: Tidal Achievement: 100 Installed
content: 100 tidal installations! This network harnesses the power of the Moon itself, delivering clean and utterly predictable electricity that will keep generating long after other technologies need replacement.

title: Nuclear Power: High-Density Energy
content: Nuclear power plants generate enormous quantities of electricity through controlled fission reactions. They deliver consistent baseload power with zero carbon emissions during operation, though they require significant upfront investment and stable geological sites. Modern designs place safety and passive cooling at the forefront.

title: Nuclear Technology Tier 2
content: Tier 2 reactors introduce improved fuel rod cladding and enhanced primary coolant chemistry controls. These upgrades extend fuel cycles and reduce radioactive waste volume per unit of energy generated.

title: Nuclear Technology Tier 3
content: Tier 3 designs adopt passive safety systems that use gravity and natural convection - not pumps or operator action - to keep the reactor cool during any conceivable fault. Safety becomes an intrinsic property of the physics, not an add-on.

title: Nuclear Technology Tier 4
content: Tier 4 plants use mixed-oxide fuel that incorporates reprocessed plutonium alongside uranium. Burning waste from earlier reactor generations reduces the long-term storage burden while extracting additional energy.

title: Nuclear Technology Tier 5
content: Tier 5 introduces small modular reactors (SMRs) that are factory-built and shipped to site. Standardised construction slashes costs and timescales, making nuclear viable in locations where a large plant would be impractical.

title: Nuclear Technology Tier 6
content: Tier 6 reactors operate at higher temperatures using molten salt coolant instead of water. The elevated thermal efficiency extracts more electricity from the same amount of fuel, and the physics make a runaway reaction impossible.

title: Nuclear Technology Tier 7
content: Tier 7 fast neutron reactors can use spent fuel from conventional reactors as their primary input. In effect, today's nuclear waste becomes tomorrow's fuel, dramatically reducing the scale of long-term storage requirements.

title: Nuclear Technology Tier 8
content: Tier 8 plants integrate thermochemical hydrogen production alongside electricity generation. Surplus heat from the reactor splits water molecules, producing clean hydrogen fuel as a valuable by-product.

title: Nuclear Technology Tier 9
content: Tier 9 designs use thorium fuel cycles that produce far less long-lived waste than uranium and carry proliferation-resistant properties. The thorium fuel cycle represents a fundamentally safer path for long-term nuclear energy.

title: Nuclear Technology Tier 10
content: Tier 10 plants incorporate compact fusion-fission hybrid cores that blend the two technologies for unprecedented energy density and near-zero long-lived waste. The energy from a single plant can power a mid-sized city indefinitely.

title: Nuclear Achievement: 25 Installed
content: 25 nuclear plants operational. Their combined steady output is providing a reliable carbon-free baseload that makes the rest of your renewable portfolio far more viable.

title: Nuclear Achievement: 50 Installed
content: 50 nuclear installations running. At this scale, your nuclear fleet is anchoring the entire grid, absorbing demand spikes that would otherwise require fossil fuel backup.

title: Nuclear Achievement: 75 Installed
content: 75 nuclear plants - a remarkable infrastructure commitment to zero-carbon baseload power. The carbon savings from this fleet alone are measurable at a national level.

title: Nuclear Achievement: 100 Installed
content: 100 nuclear installations! This is one of the largest clean energy deployments ever achieved. The combination of nuclear baseload and your renewable assets has fundamentally decarbonised the grid.

title: Energy Storage Tier 1
content: Tier 1 battery banks provide 1000 units of additional energy storage using lithium-ion cell technology. Storing surplus solar and wind output allows you to supply demand during low-generation periods rather than curtailing clean energy.

title: Energy Storage Tier 2
content: Tier 2 batteries provide 2000 units of storage using improved lithium iron phosphate chemistry. These cells tolerate deeper discharge cycles without degradation, giving more usable capacity over a longer service life.

title: Energy Storage Tier 3
content: Tier 3 storage provides 3000 units using solid-state electrolytes that eliminate flammable liquid components. The technology is safer, charges faster, and retains over 90% capacity after thousands of charge cycles.

title: Energy Storage Tier 4
content: Tier 4 systems provide 4000 units of storage via grid-scale flow batteries. Liquid electrolyte tanks can be expanded independently of the power electronics, making capacity upgrades straightforward and cost-effective.

title: Energy Storage Tier 5
content: Tier 5 installations provide 5000 units using advanced sodium-ion chemistry. Sodium is thousands of times more abundant than lithium, dramatically reducing material cost and supply-chain risk at grid scale.

title: Energy Storage Tier 6
content: Tier 6 storage provides 6000 units with AI-driven charge management that predicts grid demand and renewable generation 24 hours ahead. Intelligent scheduling maximises the value of every stored kilowatt-hour.

title: Energy Storage Tier 7
content: Tier 7 systems provide 7000 units through compressed-air energy storage in geological formations. Surplus electricity compresses air into underground caverns; expanding air drives turbines when demand peaks.

title: Energy Storage Tier 8
content: Tier 8 installations provide 8000 units using gravity storage: electric motors lift massive weights during surplus periods, then generators recover the energy as those weights descend. Simple physics at industrial scale.

title: Energy Storage Tier 9
content: Tier 9 storage provides 9000 units through liquid metal batteries operating at high temperature. Their extreme cycle durability and low degradation rate make them ideal for daily charge-discharge over decades.

title: Energy Storage Tier 10
content: Tier 10 represents next-generation supercapacitor-battery hybrids delivering 10000 units of storage. Near-instantaneous charge and discharge response allows the system to stabilise grid frequency in real time, replacing fossil peaker plants entirely.
